help: <axis> is a structure containing attributes for the axes of a plot.

min.help: minimum value for that axis in user coordinates. It is calculated.

max.help: maximum value for that axis in user coordinates. It is calculated.

step.help: the step to use between min and max for that axis. By default, it is calculated to set a tick for each value in the dataset

auto_scale.help: if set to False, both the attributes min and max need to be specified. The user does not normally need to set that attribute

scale.help: type of scale to be used along that axis: linear, logarithmic or symlog, please refer to matplotlib

horizontal.help: this is used internally to know if the axis is the xaxis or the yaxis

min_bound_padding.help: this determines the padding to add below the min value of the axis, it is a percentage of max-min. If min = 0 and max = 10, the actual min will be -1, set it to 0 to get the axis to start at 0

max_bound_padding.help: this determines the padding to add over the max value of the axis, it is a percentage of max-min. If min = 0 and max = 10, the actual max will be 11.

absolute_max.help: this ensures that the maximum value of the axis will be at least that value
